persons.arguments_against: The corpus has 4 Haddon-Smiths in staff lists and 3 biographies, so the counter-hypothesis is a same-name relative (e.g. a son) holding the Windward Islands governorship. This fails: the governorship is a unique office; the biography records 'gov., Windward Is., 14th Dec., 1914' and 'assumed govt., 30th Dec., 1914', precisely matching the stint's first listing in 1915; the forename and initial 'George B.' match 'Geo. Babil'; both carry K.C.M.G., conferred 1915, exactly when the stint begins showing it; and the bio's 'ret., 1924.' matches the stint ending its 1915-1925 listing window (the 1925 edition reflecting 1924 service). A hypothetical second Sir George B. Haddon-Smith, K.C.M.G., governing the same colony in the same years while this biography claims that exact post is not realistic. The position_sim FAIL is mechanical: the abbreviation 'gov.' scored 21 against 'Governor, Windward Islands', but they denote the same office.

evidence_cited.fact: Governor and Commander-in-Chief of the Windward Islands

persons.would_overturn: Evidence of a second George B. Haddon-Smith, K.C.M.G., as Governor of the Windward Islands in 1914-1925, or a record showing Geo. Babil Haddon-Smith declined or never assumed the 1914 appointment.

notes: Five independent agreeing dimensions: unique gubernatorial office, colony, appointment date (Dec. 1914) immediately preceding the 1915 listing, shared K.C.M.G. with matching conferral year, matching forename 'George'/'Geo.' and middle initial B (Babil), and retirement 1924 closing the listing window. The position_sim failure is an abbreviation artifact ('gov.' vs 'Governor, Windward Islands').
